title: jmap: Chat/get, Space/get, SpaceInvite/get accept unbounded ids array — N individual DB queries per call
description:
chat_get, space_get, and space_invite_get in daemon/src/jmap.rs accept an arbitrary-length ids array with no cap. Each id causes a separate sqlx query in the store (get_chats, get_spaces, get_space_invites iterate one query per id). By contrast, contact_get caps at 4096 and message_get caps at 4096.

A caller with the local API token can send Chat/get with e.g. 100,000 ids and trigger 100,000 individual DB round-trips in a single request, causing request latency to grow linearly and potentially blocking the sqlx pool for other requests.

Affected handlers (daemon/src/jmap.rs):
  - chat_get (~line 771): no ids.len() > N check before passing to get_chats
  - space_get (~line 941): no ids.len() > N check before passing to get_spaces
  - space_invite_get (~line 1605): no ids.len() > N check before passing to get_space_invites

Fix: add ids.len() > 4096 guard (matching contact_get and message_get) before constructing the id_strs slice and calling the store.
